reasoning: Aaron Peirsol's 200m backstroke Long Course World Record of 1:51.92 has stood since July 2009. It was set during the 'super-suit' era, making it notoriously difficult to beat. The current top active swimmers, like Hubert Kos (who recently broke the Short Course record) and Thomas Ceccon, have personal bests in the long course 200m backstroke that are more than a full second away from Peirsol's mark (e.g., Kos at 1:53.19). It is highly unlikely that any swimmer drops over a second to break this 16-year-old record in the short 3-month window from December 2025 to March 2026. Furthermore, major long course competitions usually happen in the summer, whereas the winter schedule predominantly focuses on short course meets. Thus, there are virtually no prime opportunities to attempt long course records over this period. I assign extremely high probability to the record surviving, decreasing slightly over time to account for the minuscule chance of sporadic long course attempts.
